data.title: Australian Mines Defines Areas for Exploration From Drilling Results at Brazil Project; Shares Rise 10%
data.description:
Australian Mines reported assay drilling results from its Jequie rare earth element project in Brazil and defined two areas for follow-up exploration to expand the footprint of known total rare earth oxide enrichment, according to a Friday Australian bourse filing.

The firm completed an auger drilling program, comprising 72 shallow auger drill holes, totaling 500.6 meters over two priority rare earth element-in-soil targets.

The results included a "significant" intercept of 15 meters at 1,720 parts-per-million (ppm) grade of total rare earth oxides, including 3 meters at 3,055 ppm grade of total rare earth oxides. The company said 69% of the assays returned total rare earth oxide values greater than 400 ppm.

The firm's shares rose 10% in recent trading on Friday.

data.title: Pulse Seismic Declares Special Dividend of C$0.20 >PSD.T
South Korea's ICT Exports Decline 0.4% in January
data.description:
South Korea's exports of information and communications technology goods fell 0.4% to $16.3 billion in January from a year earlier, the Ministry of Trade, Industry and Energy said Thursday.

Imports of ICT goods stood at $13.5 billion, which led to a trade balance surplus of $2.8 billion in the month, the release said.

Semiconductor exports rose 7.7% year-on-year to $10.1 billion in January, driven by AI demand and a 17.2% increase in memory chips to $6.2 billion. However, outbound shipments of system chips declined 4.3% to $3.5 billion.

Computer and peripheral exports climbed 10% to $0.9 billion, with solid-state drives up 27.1% to $600 million, marking 13 months of growth, according to the news release.

data.title: Santander Refutes Report of Potential Sale of UK Arm
data.description:
Banco Santander said its UK business is not for sale, Reuters reported Thursday, citing a spokesperson for the Spanish lender.

The bank made the statement after London's Financial Times reported the same day that it had early-stage talks with NatWest Group in 2024 regarding the potential divestment of its retail business in the UK. The FT report cited people familiar with the matter.

data.title: Eneos aims to raise as much as $3 bln via metals unit IPO
data.description:
JX Advanced Metals (JXAM), a wholly owned metals unit of Japan's Eneos Holdings , won approval to list its shares in a $3 billion share offering, a regulatory filing showed on Friday, in what would be the nation's biggest IPO in seven years.

JXAM has set a tentative offering price of 862 yen ($5.64) per share ahead of its listing on the Tokyo Stock Exchange on March 19.

The final pricing is set for March 10.

Eneos, Japan's largest oil refiner, plans to sell 465.2 million shares of JXAM with an overallotment option of up to 69.8 million shares. The share sale would help Eneos raise as much as 461 billion yen ($3.01 billion), according to Reuters' calculations.

($1 = 152.8600 yen)

data.title: Malaysia Q4 Foreign Direct Investment Largest in A Year
data.description:
Malaysia's foreign direct investment (FDI) increased to MYR 18.38 billion in Q4 of 2024 from MYR 14.52 billion in Q3, marking the largest amount since Q4 of 2023.

Most of the FDI flowed into the services sector, predominantly in the information & communication sub-sector and Financial activities.

This was followed by manufacturing, mining & quarrying, and agriculture sectors.

The main sources of FDI were the United States of America, Singapore, and Hong Kong.

data.title: United Breweries' Consolidated Profit Declines in Fiscal Q3
data.description:
United Breweries reported a sharp decline in its consolidated attributable profit to 384.0 million Indian rupees in the fiscal third quarter ended Dec. 31, 2024, against 853.4 million rupees a year ago.

Earnings per share contracted to 1.45 rupees compared with the year-ago figure of 3.23 rupees, according to a Thursday filing to the Indian bourses by the beermaker.

Revenue from operations in fiscal Q3, however, increased to 44.3 billion rupees from 41.6 billion rupees a year earlier.

Shares of the company were up over 2% in recent trade.

data.title: SGX Sicom May RSS3 Rubber Up 2.9 Cents at 238.0 U.S. Cents/Kg
SGX Sicom March RSS3 Rubber Untraded at Midday
SGX Sicom Benchmark March TSR20 Rubber Up 6.2 Cents at 204.8 U.S. Cents/Kg
New Zealand Shares Rise 0.6%; Complete Weekly Gain — Market Talk
data.description: New Zealand's NZX-50 closed 0.6% higher at 12989.18 as the benchmark index snapped a run of three weekly declines. Large-cap stocks and local shares of Australian banks finished higher on Friday, helping the NZX-50 add 0.7% over the week. It is now flat so far this month and 0.9% lower in 2025. The index spent all but a few minutes of Friday in positive territory, building on a strong lead by U.S. equities and finishing at a session high after New Zealand's manufacturing PMI moved into positive territory for the first time in almost two years. Medical-device maker Fisher & Paykel Healthcare was the best large-cap performer, adding 1.9%. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

data.title: CelcomDigi Target Price Raised to MYR4.10 From MYR3.82 by Kenanga IB >6947.KU
MA Financial Group's Unit Completes AU$700 Million Residential Mortgage-Backed Securities Issuance
data.description:
MA Financial Group said its residential mortgage lending unit, MA Money, has completed its Residential Mortgage-Backed Securities (RMBS) public term issuance via a AU$700 million transaction, according to a Friday Australian bourse filing.

The transaction, arranged by National Australia Bank with NAB, HSBC, Macquarie, SMBC, and Westpac acting as joint lead managers, was the "tightest-priced" inaugural Prime Borrower RMBS deal since around the COVID-19 pandemic, the filing added.

Shares of MA Financial Group rose 2% in recent trade.

data.title: Market Chatter: Hong Kong to Loosen IPO Requirements for Mainland Chinese Companies
data.description:
Financial regulators in Hong Kong will loosen requirements for the initial public offerings of mainland Chinese companies, the South China Morning Post reported Thursday, citing Secretary for Financial Services and the Treasury Christopher Hui Ching-yu.

The new regulations are seen to help Chinese companies utilize Hong Kong as a venue to fund their global expansion, the report said.

Both the Securities and Futures Commission and the Hong Kong Exchanges and Clearing will fine-tune existing regulations later in 2025 to lower the funding threshold, boost market structure, and improve corporate governance, the report said.

"We hope to further facilitate mainland companies, including firms in Shenzhen, to raise capital," the SCMP quoted Hui as saying during a conference in Shenzhen, China, the report said.

There are about 100 companies waiting to list in Hong Kong, the SCMP said, citing remarks by HKEX Chief Executive Officer Bonnie Chan Yiting made in January.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: VP Bank Raises Industrial Metals Exposure as Inflation Hedge — Interview
data.description:
By Jiahui Huang

The start of President Trump's second term has been a whirlwind for markets, presenting both risks and opportunities, and industrial metals and gold could be safe havens as hedges against potentially higher inflation, according to VP Bank.

"Don't bet only on U.S. equities," Felix Brill, chief investment officer at Liechtenstein-based VP Bank told The Wall Street Journal on Tuesday. "It all comes down to our investment philosophy: we don't like to have too many concentrated bets."

Even though U.S. equities were among the best performing asset classes last year and investors have been optimistic about U.S. stocks, they should keep in mind that Trump's tariffs could drive up inflation and undermine the market's performance.

Brill suggested current conditions echoed the pattern of inflation seen between 1968 to 1984, when inflation surged amid the 1970s oil shock. "Inflation is the one topic we have to think about this year," Brill said.

Trump's immigration policy could lead to higher wages and consumer prices, he noted, adding that tariffs are "basically nothing but a tax on consumers," which companies will pass on to consumers to some extent.

U.S. consumer-price data from the Labor Department this week showed inflation for January coming in at a larger-than-expected 3%, compared with the same period a year earlier.

As a hedge against inflation, VP Bank included industrial metals to its portfolio in January, with a 2% allocation, on top of its gold holdings, which makes up 3% of the portfolio, Brill said.

"We see a strong case that demand for industrial metals structurally and fundamentally will be improving, because you need a lot of these metals, for instance, for the energy transition," he said.

The energy sector's shift from fossil fuels as well as the rise of artificial intelligence and cloud computing could boost metal prices, he added, as supply is relatively restricted while demand remains high.

Metal prices in the U.S. have also received a recent boost after Trump said he would impose 25% tariffs on imports of aluminum and steel.

Still, the U.S. remains the bank's preferred country to invest in, with a particular interest in U.S. midcap stocks.

"On the day of the election, we bought the U.S. midcap," Brill said, adding that these companies would benefit the most from Trump's policies to boost the local economy.

The bank maintained its portfolio weighting for emerging markets. "We haven't really changed the portfolio yet, but we have already had a decent amount of emerging market exposure in our portfolios, both on the bond side, but especially on the equity side," he said, adding that both are core building blocks for the bank's portfolio.

He called Chinese AI startup DeepSeek "a game changer" to the belief that the U.S. would dominate global technology with its facilities and mammoth tech companies. Still, "at the moment, we have not increased the exposure, especially to Chinese tech."

VP Bank thinks China remains a value play for investment and sees some green shoots in the property sector and consumption. Still, the bank remains in a wait-and-see mode ahead of China's National People's Congress meeting in March, when major economic policies from Beijing could be announced.

As of the first half of 2024, VP Bank's client assets under management was around 50.4 billion Swiss francs, or equivalent to $55.81 billion.

Write to Jiahui Huang at jiahui.huang@wsj.com

Australian gold stocks hit record high as bullion shines
data.description:
** Australian gold stocks rise as much as 1.8% to 10,383.800, a record high, last up 1.3%

** Stocks helped by underlying bullion prices, which are now set for a seventh weekly gain as investors flock to safe haven gold over concerns of a global trade war

** Evolution mining is up as much as 2.4% to hit its highest since November 9, 2020, while Northern Star Resources is up 0.7%

** rose 13.1% in 2024

data.title: Greentown China To Issue $350 Million Senior Bonds
data.description:
Greentown China Holdings plans to issue $350 million senior bonds on Feb. 25, according to a Friday filing with the Hong Kong bourse.

The bonds will have an 8.45% interest rate and will mature in 2028. The funds will be used for the retirement of existing debt.

data.title: Lincotrade and Associates' Attributable Profit Slides 60% in Fiscal H1
data.description:
Lincotrade and Associates' profit attributable to owners of the company slid 60% to SG$717,000 for six months ended Dec. 31, 2024, from SG$1.8 million a year earlier, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on Thursday.

Earnings per share dropped to SG$0.0042 compared with SG$0.0104 in the comparable period.

Revenue in fiscal H1 declined 21% year over year to SG$33.7 million from SG$42.7 million, mainly due to decreased revenue across all major business segments.

data.title: GuocoLand's Subsidiary Secures Singapore Land Parcel with SG$628 Million Bid
data.description:
GuocoLand's indirect wholly owned subsidiary, GLL B, was awarded a land parcel at River Valley Green by the Urban Redevelopment Authority of Singapore for a bid price of SG$627.8 million.

The 99-year leasehold parcel has a gross plot ratio of 3.5 and a land area of 11,736 square meters, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on Thursday.

The company plans to develop the parcel into a high-end waterfront development comprising two towers with commercial shops located at the first story.

data.title: Eneos: Additional 69.8M JX Advanced Metals Shares May Be Offered
Ringgit May Trade in 4.42-4.47 Range Versus Dollar Next Week — Market Talk
data.description: The Malaysian ringgit is expected to trade between 4.42 and 4.47 against the U.S. dollar next week, supported by stable domestic macro indicators, Kenanga economists say in a note. Markets now anticipate one Fed rate cut in September this year, though Kenanga still sees two cuts contingent on Trump's tariff policies and potential retaliation. While U.S. reciprocal tariffs are delayed until April, risk sentiment remains cautious, they say. Traders will monitor Russia-Ukraine geopolitical developments, U.S. retail sales data as key drivers for the pair, they add. Kenanga pegs USD/MYR's short-term resistance at 4.459 and support at 4.425. USD/MYR is 0.3% lower at 4.4420. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)
